title: (UP TO $12000) Change Management Specialist I Enterprise AI Transformation    #TNKA
description:
Our client is a major banking firm

Job Summary

The Change Management Specialist will support the Talent and Culture track of the Bank's enterprise AI transformation program. This role focuses on driving adoption of advanced AI solutions, managing communications, upskilling, and workforce impacts, and enabling Business Units to execute change effectively.

12 months
Raffles Place
Weekdays office hours
9-12k/mth, No completion bonus

Responsibilities

  • Lead the implementation of change strategies to drive adoption of advanced AI solutions across the bank, ensuring alignment with business objectives and technology rollout plans
  • Collaborate with HR to integrate change implications into people and change strategies, workforce planning, and job architecture design
  • Conduct change impact assessments across Business Units to identify effects on roles, skills, workflows, governance, and controls
  • Support execution of workforce transition plans, including role evolution, redeployment pathways, and reskilling initiatives to enable smooth workforce transformation
  • Design and deploy change management artefacts such as playbooks, toolkits, leader talking points, and adoption dashboards to facilitate engagement and enablement
  • Partner with HR, Learning & Development, and external vendors to support upskilling and retraining programs that build capability readiness
  • Coordinate with senior management, Business Unit leaders, and external consultants (e.g., Accenture) to ensure strategic coherence and effective delivery of change initiatives
  • Develop clear, credible, and consistent change narratives to communicate transformation objectives and progress to stakeholders
  • Define and track change effectiveness and adoption metrics, including usage, behaviour change, and capability readiness, to measure impact and inform continuous improvement
  • Adapt change management approaches based on feedback and data insights to optimize outcomes in a fast-evolving AI environment

Required competencies and certifications

  • Minimum 8 years of experience in change management, organisational transformation, or workforce transformation
  • Proven track record supporting large-scale technology or digital transformations within banking or financial services environments
  • Strong grounding in change management methodologies (e.g., Prosci, Kotter, ADKAR) with pragmatic application in complex enterprise settings
  • Experience in change impact analysis, target operating model design, and workforce transition planning
  • Ability to connect technology changes to people, capability, and operating model outcomes through strong analytical and synthesis skills
  • Experience working in enterprise environments with multiple stakeholders
  • Executive-level communication skills, both written and verbal, with strong influencing capabilities without formal authority

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Hands-on experience with AI, automation, data, or advanced analytics programs
  • Background in consulting combined with in-house delivery experience
  • Experience collaborating with HR on enterprise people strategy or skills transformation
  • Certification in change management (Prosci, ACMP, or equivalent)


Frederick Tan
The Outsourcing Team
Recruit Express Pte Ltd
EA Lic: 99C4599

postedCompany.description:
Established since 1996, Recruit Express has emerged as the leading staffing solutions provider that has set a proven track record for matching the right people to the right organization within the shortest time. With our vast network of resources, extensive databases and defined recruitment processes, we have been successfully bridging talented job seekers of the highest calibre to employers who only want the best in their teams.


In our relentless pursuit of excellent service, we have adopted best practices and dynamic growth strategies in expanding our operations across the Asia Pacific regions: We have offices in Singapore, Sydney, Hong Kong, Kuala Lumpur, Taipei, Shanghai, Beijing, Tokyo and Bangkok.

With 400 permanent committed consultants from various professional backgrounds and disciplines, we make a difference by delivering top-notch services to our clients and candidates alike.
